summary: VEIL Apple App Store compliance pre-submission test. Backend: 19/20 pytest tests PASS, 1 skipped (premium-unlock visual cannot be verified because review@veil.app has 0 received taps). Frontend: Welcome, login, grid, user detail, profile menu, register, all 4 legal pages, premium screen all render correctly with proper mobile UX. All Apple compliance gates verified: age<18 rejected, delete account is real, distance rounded to 0.5km, prohibited terms blocked, rate limit 429 at 30/min, block removes from nearby, report saved with category, 18+ checkbox + Terms/Privacy on register, premium shows 'Próximamente' (no external payments).

minor.issue: Seed does NOT auto-create review@veil.app premium account; it currently exists from prior manual creation. If DB is wiped, App Review will fail to log in. Recommend adding review account creation inside /api/seed (idempotent, is_premium=true).

Because review@veil.app was not created via register flow, it has 0 auto-generated taps. Apple reviewer logging in sees empty tap screen and cannot visually verify premium unlock feature. Recommend seeding 8-10 received taps for review@veil.app on /seed.

issues: INTEREST_GROUPS contains 64 interests across 8 groups, NOT the required 75+. Distribution: Estilo de vida (12), Cultura (9), Música (9), Viajes (7), Vida nocturna (6), Hogar (8), Profesional (7), Conexión (6). Add ~11 more items to reach 75+.

Stale test profiles visible in nearby feed ('Guzman , 24', 'Test User, 28', 'Guzman, 25') with empty avatars from prior pytest runs. Clean up before App Store submission so Apple reviewer sees only curated demo profiles.

react_native_issues.issue: Uses Alert.alert with multiple option buttons. React Native Web only renders the first button — meaning the report category picker (Acoso/Suplantación/Contenido sexual/Spam/Menor/Otro) and the Bloquear confirmation are NOT visually testable on web preview. Works correctly on native iOS, so Apple App Review (real device) is unaffected. Backend POST /api/report verified via pytest. Consider migrating to a custom Modal for cross-platform parity.

action_items: MEDIUM: Add review@veil.app creation to POST /api/seed so account is recreated if DB is wiped, plus seed 8-10 demo taps for it so premium-unlock UX is demonstrable on the very first login.
MEDIUM: Expand INTEREST_GROUPS in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/profile.tsx from 64 to 75+ items (currently 64). Suggest adding to thinner groups (Música +6, Vida nocturna +5, Conexión +5, Viajes +3).

LOW: Clean up stale test accounts ('Guzman', 'Test User', etc.) from MongoDB before App Store submission so reviewer sees curated demo data only. Run: db.users.deleteMany({email: {$regex: '^TEST_|^auto_'}}) or similar.
LOW (post-launch quality): Replace Alert.alert with custom Modal for report/block flows so multi-option pickers also work on web preview (currently iOS-only).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py user_to_public is_boosted ternary is hard to read (line 137). Works but consider extracting to helper.
/api/boost/activate is not gated on premium/payment — anyone can boost (acknowledged in code comment). Fine for demo, but flag for future revenue work.
Auto-generated tap timestamps use random.randint(1, 120) minutes which means some auto-taps could appear as 2 hours old — fine, just be aware.

success_rate.backend: 95% (19/20 passed, 1 skipped due to data gap)

seed_data_creation: Used existing review@veil.app + demo1..demo26@veil.app (already in DB, /api/seed reports existing=45). Created several TEST_* throwaway accounts during pytest that self-clean via DELETE /api/auth/account.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_veil_api.py is the regression suite — run with: cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. All 19 active tests are passing. The one skipped test (test_premium_user_sees_all_unlocked) will start passing once review@veil.app has ≥1 received tap in DB. Stale test users in /api/users/nearby grid are visual noise from previous iterations — not a code bug.
rca_of_issue: No P0/P1 issues. The two MEDIUM gaps (review account not in /seed, interests count 64 vs 75+ required) are content/data issues, not code bugs. Both are quick fixes for the main agent.
